perturbation.original_text: Hearing Aids are amplification devices primarily designed to compensate for hearing loss, designed for listening only (through a microphone, telecoil, Direct Audio Input or other similar audio pathways and run on a low power hybrid circuit. Hearing Aids may include in-the- ear Hearing Aids (ITE), behind-the-ear Hearing Aids (BTE), or completely-in-the-canal Hearing Aids (CIC).
perturbation.changed_text: Hearing Aids are amplification devices primarily designed to compensate for hearing loss, designed for listening only (through a microphone, telecoil, Direct Audio Input or other similar audio pathways and run on a high power digital circuit. Hearing Aids only include behind-the-ear Hearing Aids (BTE).
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by altering the definition of 'Hearing Aids'. The original text states they run on a low power hybrid circuit, while the altered text specifies 'high power digital circuit' and restricts the types of hearing aids to only Behind-the-ear. This change creates a contradiction with later parts of the agreement that assume Hearing Aids may include other types, creating an in-text contradiction with 'Hearing Aids may include in-the- ear Hearing Aids (ITE), behind-the-ear Hearing Aids (BTE), or completely-in-the-canal Hearing Aids (CIC)' in the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Hearing Aids are amplification devices primarily designed to compensate for hearing loss, designed for listening only (through a microphone, telecoil, Direct Audio Input or other similar audio pathways and run on a high power digital circuit. Hearing Aids only include behind-the-ear Hearing Aids (BTE).
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the definition of 'Hearing Aids' by specifying 'high power digital circuit' instead of 'low power hybrid circuit' and restricting the types to 'behind-the-ear Hearing Aids (BTE)' only. This directly contradicts the original text, where Hearing Aids 'may include in-the- ear Hearing Aids (ITE), behind-the-ear Hearing Aids (BTE), or completely-in-the-canal Hearing Aids (CIC)'. Because the definition of Hearing Aids has changed from potentially including ITE and CIC to only including BTE, there is a direct contradiction. This is an example of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction because the definition of a key term is inconsistent, which could make the agreement difficult to enforce.

perturbation.original_text: Base Product means the DSP Technology including the ON Semiconductor Ezairo or SCHA chips, the EEPROM and the firmware loaded onto the EEPROM and the minimum printed circuit board package necessary to connect and house these items. The Base Product does not include additional PCB or flex circuits necessary to connect to other components of an Enhanced Product.
perturbation.changed_text: Base Product means ONLY the DSP Technology including the ON Semiconductor Ezairo or SCHA chips, the EEPROM and the firmware loaded onto the EEPROM. The Base Product include additional PCB or flex circuits necessary to connect to other components of an Enhanced Product.
perturbation.explanation: By specifying that the 'Base Product' means 'ONLY' the listed DSP technology and contradicting 'The Base Product does not include additional PCB or flex circuits necessary to connect to other components of an Enhanced Product' to now include them, and introduces ambiguity. Later sections rely on a clear distinction between Base and Enhanced Products, particularly regarding royalty payments and licensing terms. A contradiction is caused between the new definition and the original use of the term Base Product Unit later in section 4.9 where it states 'Royalty Payments will be made only on the Base Product Unit, or on the Base Product portion of an Enhanced Product for any Base Product included in an Enhanced Product'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Royalty Payments will be made only on the Base Product Unit, or on the Base Product portion of an Enhanced Product for any Base Product included in an Enhanced Product. The initial base rate of royalty shall include the use of the DSP Framework. In no case shall IntriCon pay Royalty Payments on any product or portion of any product other than for revenue received for Base Product Unit or for the Base Product portion of an Enhanced Product, and in the case a Base Product portion of an Enhanced Product such Base Product portion shall not be given a value that is influenced by its inclusion in the Enhanced Product. Royalty Payment for HH, ALD and PADA units that incorporate Dynamic Hearing Technology shall be paid according to the table set forth below:
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Base Product' to include additional PCB or flex circuits, directly contradicting the original definition which specifically excluded them. This creates a direct conflict with the 'Contradicted Text' which states that royalty payments are made only on the 'Base Product Unit' or the 'Base Product portion of an Enhanced Product'. By changing the definition of what constitutes a 'Base Product', the new definition directly alters what royalty payments are due. This is a strong contradiction that would lead to disputes over contractual obligations.

perturbation.original_text: IntriCon must keep accurate and complete records of orders received, Base Product Units / DSP Features/Modules Sold and returned and all other records reasonably necessary to substantiate all Royalty Payments to be made to Dynamic Hearing under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: IntriCon will keep records of all orders received, but is NOT obligated to keep records of DSP Features/Modules Sold and returned and other records reasonably necessary to substantiate all Royalty Payments to be made to Dynamic Hearing under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This alteration directly contradicts the original obligation for IntriCon to maintain comprehensive records related to sales and returns of DSP features/modules which are crucial for accurate royalty calculation. By explicitly stating that IntriCon is 'NOT obligated' to keep these records, it introduces significant ambiguity and conflict. This contradiction is evident when considering section 5.1, which requires IntriCon to provide a statement of the actual number and value of Base Product Units Sold along with the number and value of DSP features/models sold with those Base Product Units.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Within thirty (30) days of the end of each payment quarter IntriCon must provide to Dynamic Hearing a statement of the actual number and value of Base Product Units Sold along with the number and value of DSP features/models sold with those Base Product Units by it or any Related Body Corporate. The statement must also include calculations of the per unit royalty in accordance with clause 4.9. Any royalty due for payment by IntriCon to Dynamic Hearing is payable at the same time the statement is provided.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states IntriCon is 'NOT obligated' to keep records of DSP Features/Modules Sold and returned. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires IntriCon to provide a statement including the number and value of DSP features/models sold. The change makes it impossible to fulfill the obligation in the contradicted text because the necessary records are explicitly not required to be kept.
